text:
Market today: The market's mood is cautious on tech, especially chips and AI. Samsung posted huge profits, but investors focused on future AI spending concerns, leading to selling. This shows skepticism even with good news.

Your holdings: SOXL is a clear SELL, down -23.52%, and needs immediate attention as it's below its default stop. SPAL, a new leveraged ETF, is also down -11.17% and nearing its stop. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on SOXL's stop and the general market sentiment around AI spending. Any further negative news here could hit your concentrated tech book hard.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your leveraged and underperforming tech positions, especially given the market's current skepticism.
plays: SOXL: Your default stop of 200 has been breached. Re-evaluate your risk tolerance and decide on an exit strategy immediately to protect capital.
SPAL: Watch its stop at 21.38 closely. As a leveraged ETF, it moves fast, and margin amplifies losses.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The market's current sentiment on AI spending suggests caution before adding new positions.
